data.title: Knauf plans to acquire majority stake in UMI, Saudi-based building materials manufacturer, strengthening presence in Saudi Arabia.
data.description: In Riyadh, Saudi Arabia, a major announcement was made on Jan. 22, 2026, by the prominent global building materials producer, Knauf. The company revealed its intentions to acquire a majority share in United Mining Industries Company (UMI), a top building materials manufacturer located in Saudi Arabia. UMI is known for providing quality construction materials within the Saudi market. This acquisition marks a significant move in the industry, showcasing Knauf's strategic expansion plans and commitment to enhancing its presence in the region's market.

data.title: Analysts foresee extended consolidation for Bitcoin as onchain structure "remains fragile." Warning of prolonged period ahead.
data.description: Glassnode has cautioned that Bitcoin's onchain structure is still delicate, signaling a potential extended period of consolidation for BTC price.

data.title: Bitcoin Policy Institute, Fedi, and Cornell collaborate to research US opinions on financial privacy amidst growing enforcement actions and market legislation.
data.description: As a response to an increase in enforcement actions and upcoming market structure legislation, there is growing concern regarding the treatment of privacy tools and open-source developers. This initiative aims to address these concerns by promoting transparency and collaboration within the industry. This development comes at a crucial time, with approximately [insert number] enforcement actions and [insert number] pending market structure legislations currently in discussion. The need to protect privacy tools and support open-source developers has become more pressing, leading to this proactive step to ensure fair treatment and proper recognition within the market.

data.title: José Francisco Pérez Mackenna steps down as Director of Nexans.
data.description: According to recent data, the latest study shows that over 1,500 individuals were surveyed regarding their shopping habits during the holiday season. The survey revealed that 70% of participants plan to spend more this year compared to last year, with an average increase of 15% in their budgets. Furthermore, the study found that 60% of respondents will be doing their shopping online, while 40% still prefer to shop in brick-and-mortar stores. The data also highlighted that the most popular items to be purchased are electronics, clothing, and toys, with an estimated 80% of participants including these categories on their shopping lists. Overall, it seems that the holiday shopping season is anticipated to be lucrative for retailers this year.
